completionNotes: Completed as a free-form query suggestion pattern with recent suggestions, typed-query suggestions, explicit search submission, dismiss behavior, autocomplete distinction, source-backed evidence, and screenshot spot-check evidence.

completionEvidence.patternSpecificGuidance: Guidance names recent searches, query suggestions, typed input preservation, explicit submit, dismiss behavior, stale suggestion handling, and no-suggestion state for open-ended search.
completionEvidence.relatedPatternDistinction: The entry separates search suggestions from basic search submission, autocomplete known-value completion, and no-results recovery after a submitted query finds nothing.
completionEvidence.liveDemoBehavior: The live demo shows recent suggestions before typing, changes suggestions from typed input, lets users select a suggestion without auto-submitting, supports dismissing suggestions, and submits the final free-form query explicitly.
completionEvidence.comparisonDecisionRules: The linked search-suggestions-vs-autocomplete comparison distinguishes several possible query paths from constrained known values such as addresses, products, people, or codes.
completionEvidence.duplicateAudit: npm run audit:completion compares normalized guidance fields across patterns and currently reports no duplicate field group blocking this completed pattern.
completionEvidence.manualSpotCheck: screenshots/patterns/search-suggestions.png was inspected after rendering and shows Good UI, Bad UI, Good UX, Bad UX, suggestion rows, explicit search and dismiss controls, source-backed guidance, and comparison links.

name: Search suggestions
problem: Users need help forming a useful search query before they submit or while they refine it.
solution: Show relevant suggested queries or result hints tied to the current input, while preserving user control of the final query.
problemContext: Users may not know the exact wording used by the system.
The product can produce useful suggestions from content, history, or taxonomy.
selectionRules: Choose suggestions when guidance improves query formulation.
Keep suggestions relevant to the current input and scope.
Let users ignore suggestions and continue free-form search.
interactionContract: Suggestions update from the current input without replacing it unexpectedly.
Selecting a suggestion makes the resulting query clear.
Users can dismiss or continue typing.
implementationChecklist: Highlight matching text only when it helps recognition.
Keep keyboard and pointer selection equivalent.
commonMisuses: Auto-submitting the top suggestion without confirmation.
Showing unrelated promoted results as suggestions.
critiqueQuestions: Do suggestions help users express intent, or are they distracting from the search task?
useWhen: The system can predict likely queries.
avoidWhen: Suggestions are low quality or biased toward irrelevant content.
The input is a constrained value better served by autocomplete.
accessibility: Expose the suggestions list and active option.
Do not rely only on visual highlighting for matched text.
keyboardBehavior: Arrow keys can move through suggestions.
Enter can choose a highlighted suggestion.
Escape dismisses suggestions while keeping input.
failureModes: Stale suggestions after the query changes.
Suggestions covering the results without a dismiss path.

sources.note: Carbon search guidance supports recent searches, type-ahead suggestions, keyboard selection, Escape dismissal, and search result counts.
APG combobox guidance covers popup suggestions and keyboard operation.

uiGuidance: Render a search field with query suggestions, active option, selected suggestion, and no-suggestion state.
Keep suggestions visually tied to the current input.
uxGuidance: Help users formulate better search queries without forcing a choice.
Preserve free-form typing and avoid submitting suggestions without confirmation.

good: Suggestions appear directly below the search field with active row, readable labels, and optional matched text.
Recent, popular, or scoped suggestions are visually distinguished when mixed.
bad: Suggestions overlay unrelated content without a dismiss path.
Promoted results styled the same as typed query suggestions.

good: Users can keep typing, choose a suggestion, dismiss suggestions, or submit their own query.
Suggestions update with the current input and do not submit until confirmed.
bad: Stale suggestions remain after query changes.

examples.notes: Documents recent search suggestions, type-ahead suggestions, keyboard navigation, and explicit search behavior.
Documents popup suggestion behavior and keyboard expectations used by accessible suggestion lists.
